semantic.role: Index record for the ArchSpine mirror system, tracking the state of indexed source files, synchronization metadata, and reverse index completeness.
responsibilities: Maintain content hashes (SHA-256) for each tracked source file to detect modifications
Record synchronization timestamps and mode (full/incremental) for mirror coherence
Track the completion status of reverse index construction
Provide a count of indexed units for verification and reporting
invariants: contentHash must be exactly 64 hexadecimal characters (SHA-256 digest)
fileKind must be a valid enumerated value such as 'source' or 'doc'
sourceExists must be a boolean (true or false)
lastIndexedAt must be a valid ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone
indexedUnitCount must be a non-negative integer

English.role: This configuration file functions as the authoritative index for the ArchSpine mirror system. It records every source file that has been indexed, along with its cryptographic hash, last indexing timestamp, and existence status. The sync section logs the most recent synchronization event, including the mode (full or incremental) and whether the reverse index is fully built. The indexedUnitCount provides a quick sanity check on the total number of indexed units.

parameter_definitions.schemaVersion: Defines the version of the index schema. Changing this without migrating can break consumers expecting certain fields.
parameter_definitions.generatorVersion: Identifies the tool and version that generated this index. Useful for debugging and compatibility checks.
parameter_definitions.createdAt: Timestamp when this index was first created. Not updated on subsequent syncs; used for auditing.
parameter_definitions.updatedAt: Timestamp of the last modification to this index. Affects sync decision logic if the index is stale.
parameter_definitions.sync.lastSyncAt: Records when the last synchronization with the source repository completed. Used by orchestrators to decide if a new sync is needed.
parameter_definitions.sync.lastSyncMode: Indicates whether the last sync was a full rebuild or an incremental update. A full sync may overwrite incremental state; safety implications if modes mismatch.
parameter_definitions.sync.reverseIndexComplete: Boolean flag indicating whether the reverse index (mapping from queries to files) is fully built. If false, search and lookup operations may return incomplete results.
parameter_definitions.sync.indexedUnitCount: Total number of indexed units (files or documents). Provides a quick check for data integrity; a mismatch may indicate corruption or incomplete indexing.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.contentHash: SHA-256 hash of the file content. Used to detect changes. A mismatch between this hash and the actual file could cause stale index or incorrect mirror state.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.fileKind: Categorizes the file as 'source' or 'doc'. Affects how the file is processed in downstream pipelines (e.g., compilation vs documentation rendering).
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.lastIndexedAt: Timestamp of when the file was last indexed. Helps determine if the file needs re-indexing after modification.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.docs: Array of associated documentation references. Currently empty; may be used to link documentation to source files. Integrity impacts if not properly populated.
parameter_definitions.files.<path>.sourceExists: Boolean indicating whether the source file still exists on disk. If false, the index may be stale and require cleanup.

English.stability_and_risks: The stability of the ArchSpine mirror system depends heavily on the accuracy of the content hashes and synchronization timestamps. An incorrect hash can cause the system to miss file changes, leading to stale index data. If reverseIndexComplete is false, query operations may return incomplete or incorrect results, affecting downstream consumers like AI agents or search interfaces. The indexedUnitCount mismatch may go unnoticed if monitoring is absent, leading to silent data loss. Files marked with sourceExists: false but still present in the index can cause orphaned references. Overall, this file must be kept in strict sync with the actual source repository to ensure system integrity.
